title: pyrus communis var. ambigua
intro: Pyrus communis var. ambigua, also known as the wild service tree, is a deciduous tree native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its small, yellowish flowers and edible fruit. The bark and leaves of the wild service tree have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The medicinal properties of Pyrus communis var. ambigua include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory issues, offering health benefits such as improved gut health, boosted immune system, and relief from coughs and colds. Herbal preparations of the wild service tree include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, syrups, and salves for topical application.
